explanations.1: The ${PROJECT_NAME:-your-project-name} syntax means 'use the PROJECT_NAME variable if it exists, otherwise use the default value your-project-name'.
explanations.9: This maps port 8080 on your computer to port 80 inside the container; if WEB_PORT isn't set, it defaults to 8080.
explanations.27: The service_healthy condition means Docker will wait until the joomladb container passes its health check before starting Joomla.
explanations.28: This runs the container as a specific user ID and group ID instead of root, which is a security practice; UID and GID default to 1000 if not provided.
explanations.39: There's a typo here: it should be ${MYSQL_PORT:-3306} not ${MYSQL_PORT-:3306}, so this will always use the literal string '-:3306' instead of defaulting to 3306.
explanations.43: Setting MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD to '1' tells MySQL to generate a random root password automatically instead of using a fixed one.
explanations.46: This health check command tests whether MySQL is responding to pings; if it fails, Docker will retry up to 10 times with 20-second timeouts.
